title: Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple
tagline: Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located at Irumbai in Vanur taluk in Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located at Irumbai in Vanur taluk in Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva. He is called as Maakaleswarar. His consort is known as Kuyilmozhi Ammai.

topPlaces.name: Irumbai
topPlaces.description: Irumbai is a village situated next to Auroville in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and at about 10 km from Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Kottakarai
topPlaces.description: Kottakarai is an Indian village in Villupuram district of Tamil Nadu. The village is called Kottakarai because kottai means palace in English.

topPlaces.name: Auroville
topPlaces.description: Auroville (; City of Dawn French: Cité de l'aube) is an experimental township in Viluppuram district, mostly in the state of Tamil Nadu, India, with some parts in the Union Territory of Puducherry in India. It was founded in 1968 by Mirra Alfassa (known as "the Mother" or "La Mère") and designed by architect Roger Anger.

topPlaces.name: Matrimandir
topPlaces.description: The Matrimandir is an edifice of spiritual significance for practitioners of integral yoga in the centre of Auroville, India established by Mirra Alfassa of the Sri Aurobindo Ashram, Auroville's spiritual co-founder, known to her followers as The Mother or La Mère. It is called Soul of the City (French: L'âme de la Ville) and situated in a large open space called "Paix" (French for Peace).

topPlaces.name: Arasaleeswarar temple
topPlaces.description: Arasaleeswarar Temple (also called Arasili) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Olindiyampattu, a village in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Arasaleeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Kottakuppam
topPlaces.description: Kottakuppam is a developing town in Viluppuram district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India, approximately 5km from the nearby city of Pondicherry.

topPlaces.name: Lawspet
topPlaces.description: Lawspet is a locality in the union territory of Puducherry in India. It was named after Jean Law de Lauriston, a French military commander and cousin of John Law who was known to Mughal historians as Musa Lass.

topPlaces.name: Vanur block
topPlaces.description: The Vanur block is a revenue block in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 65 panchayat villages.

items.note: Irumbai is a village situated next to Auroville in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and at about 10 km from Pondicherry.
Kottakarai is an Indian village in Villupuram district of Tamil Nadu. The village is called Kottakarai because kottai means palace in English.

Auroville (; City of Dawn French: Cité de l'aube) is an experimental township in Viluppuram district, mostly in the state of Tamil Nadu, India, with some parts in the Union Territory of Puducherry in India. It was founded in 1968 by Mirra Alfassa (known as "the Mother" or "La Mère") and designed by architect Roger Anger.

The Matrimandir is an edifice of spiritual significance for practitioners of integral yoga in the centre of Auroville, India established by Mirra Alfassa of the Sri Aurobindo Ashram, Auroville's spiritual co-founder, known to her followers as The Mother or La Mère. It is called Soul of the City (French: L'âme de la Ville) and situated in a large open space called "Paix" (French for Peace).

Arasaleeswarar Temple (also called Arasili) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Olindiyampattu, a village in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Arasaleeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.
Kottakuppam is a developing town in Viluppuram district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India, approximately 5km from the nearby city of Pondicherry.

Lawspet is a locality in the union territory of Puducherry in India. It was named after Jean Law de Lauriston, a French military commander and cousin of John Law who was known to Mughal historians as Musa Lass.
The Vanur block is a revenue block in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 65 panchayat villages.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (117 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (131 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Irumbai Mahaleswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Irumbai, Kottakarai, Auroville, Matrimandir, Arasaleeswarar temple.
